message:
perf(types): introduce SmallText SSO to eliminate heap allocation for short strings

The vast majority of text values flowing through the VDBE and SQL function
layer are short: column names, type labels ("integer", "text", "null"),
JSON keys, UUID strings, format results, and small user data. Every one
of these previously required an Arc<str> heap allocation — a malloc, an
atomic refcount init, and eventual dealloc — even for a 3-byte string
like "wal" or an empty string "".

This commit introduces `SmallText`, a small-string-optimized text type
that stores strings <= 23 bytes inline (on the stack / in the struct)
without any heap allocation. Longer strings transparently fall back to
Arc<str> for O(1) cloning. The 23-byte inline capacity was chosen to
keep the total struct size at 24 bytes (matching Arc<str> pointer size)
while aligning with common cache line fractions.

SmallText implements all the traits SqliteValue consumers expect:
Clone, Debug, Display, PartialEq/Eq, PartialOrd/Ord, Hash, Deref<str>,
Borrow<str>, AsRef<str>, From<&str>/From<String>/From<Arc<str>>, and
Serde Serialize/Deserialize.

The SqliteValue::Text variant now wraps SmallText instead of Arc<str>.
This change propagates across every crate that constructs or pattern-
matches on Text values:

- fsqlite-types: record decode/encode, value coercion, affinity, tests
- fsqlite-ast: literal-to-rebase conversion
- fsqlite-func: all 30+ builtin scalar functions (typeof, hex, lower,
  upper, trim, substr, replace, quote, soundex, char, concat, format,
  sqlite_version, etc.)
- fsqlite-ext-fts5: highlight, snippet, source_id, cursor column
- fsqlite-ext-icu: icu_upper, icu_lower
- fsqlite-ext-json: json_extract, json_arrow, json_each, json_tree,
  json_set/insert/replace/remove/patch, json_array/object/quote/pretty
- fsqlite-ext-misc: decimal_*, uuid/uuid_str/uuid_blob
- fsqlite-ext-rtree: geopoly_json, geopoly_svg
- fsqlite-ext-session: (import adjustment)
- fsqlite-vdbe/codegen: compile_time_insert_value literal lowering
- fsqlite-vfs/memory: unused PageData import cleanup
- fsqlite-mvcc/index_regen: typeof eval, RTRIM collation
- fsqlite-wasm: (import adjustment)
- fsqlite/migrate: (import adjustment)

The record layer's serialize_record also gains a minor optimization:
when the output buffer already has sufficient capacity, it truncates
instead of clearing and resizing, avoiding a redundant zeroing pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -219,8 +219,8 @@ fn literal_to_rebase(lit: &Literal) -> Option<RebaseExpr> {
     let val = match lit {
         Literal::Integer(i) => SqliteValue::Integer(*i),
         Literal::Float(f) => SqliteValue::Float(*f),
-        Literal::String(s) => SqliteValue::Text(Arc::from(s.as_str())),
-        Literal::Blob(b) => SqliteValue::Blob(Arc::from(b.as_slice())),
+        Literal::String(s) => SqliteValue::Text(s.as_str().into()),
+        Literal::Blob(b) => SqliteValue::Blob(b.clone().into()),
         Literal::Null => SqliteValue::Null,
         Literal::True => SqliteValue::Integer(1),
         Literal::False => SqliteValue::Integer(0),
